url: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t0NW3NABvbI
title: The Macro Minute: Is the US Treasury still keenly focused on supporting asset markets?

Happy Wednesday out there team 42, it's
your skipper here at Darius Dale on our
macro minute for Wednesday, May 6, 2026.
As always, we'll start with the
executive summary from today's lead off
morning note. So, let's dive right in.
Today's key macro question is, is the US
Treasury still keenly focused on
supporting asset markets? Our answer is
yes. The Q2 QRA confirmed the Treasury's
dovish net financing policy is likely to
persist for the foreseeable future. The
Q2 QRA projects a $39 billion decrease
in nominal coupon issuance in Q3. The Q2
QRA is also calling for 200 to 400
billion per annum in indefinite net
financing support from the Fed via
reserve management purchases and MBS
principal reinvestment, which is in line
with the projections proffered in the Q1
QRA.
The TBAC meeting minutes also confirmed
our expectation for deregulatory support
for the Treasury market. Lastly, the Q2
QRA seriously entertained the idea of
the Treasury investing excess TGA cash
into the repo market, but ultimately
decided against this incrementally
dovish policy at the current juncture.
The Treasury and TBAC left the door open
to further discussion and implementation
of this liquidity supportive scheme in
the future.
Transition to our 42 macro dashboard, as
always, we'll wrap up with a question
from our community. Uh this one's
titled, "Uh if you went from broke to
wealthy through a patent sale, how would
you invest it?" It says, "Hey Darius,
hypothetically, uh and hopefully if you
went from broke to having significant
resources through a patent sale, how
would you invest it considering where we
are in the market cycle?" I would think
sitting mostly in cash and waiting for a
pullback would be part of that strategy.
I hope to have this problem uh someday.
Uh so, uh let me start before my before
I offer my answer to this question, let
me remind you guys that, you know,
nothing we say constitutes investment
advice for any individual or groups of
individuals. You know, consult our
disclaimer on our website for more
details. Uh but just kind of answering
this from the hypothetical perspective
of what would I do is the same thing I
currently do with my entire liquid net
worth, which is invest in our KISS model
portfolio.
And so, to the extent that you have a a
tremendous amount of cash and that you
or if I ever got a tremendous amount of
cash all at once, I would continue to
implement the same strategy that I've
been implementing, which is to dollar
cost average into the current
recommended recommended KISS
allocations. And the reason this is
important is because markets trend most
of the time. You can't sit there and
just wait on a pullback. That we don't
market timing does not work. No investor
in in the history has ever proven to be
able to consistently pick tops or
consistently pick bottoms in financial
markets. So, if you try to optimize your
investment process for this nonsensical
behavior, you're going to have
nonsensical results. So, your best bet
is to understand what the trend is,
listen to the wisdom of the crowd, and
respect the wisdom of the crowd. You
know, just kind of wrapping up with a
couple
a couple statistics that you I'll throw
you. If you look at the S&P 500, it's it
spends 73% of its time trending and only
3% of its time changing trend. If you
look at Bitcoin, it spends 91% of its
time trending and only 1% of its time
changing trend. So, the key takeaway is
if you sit there with a big pot of cash
waiting on a pullback that may never
materialize or not materialize on any
real time frame that is helpful for you,
then ultimately what you're going to
wind up doing is is just being left
behind in the dust by financial market
participants and suffering another
historic Cantillon effect for those of
us who are taking advantage of the
trends financial markets to make money
for our families and our clients. So,
we'll wrap it up there. Gary still here
presenting our macro minute for
Wednesday, May 6th, 2026. Best of luck
out there today. We'll catch back here
tomorrow. Cheers.
